prevalence.value: 0.3-1.0% (higher in older adults and malabsorption risk groups)
prevalence.trend: stable; detection increasing with routine CBC and B12 testing

burden.mortalityRate: Low; neurologic complications possible if untreated

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Macrocytic anemia (MCV elevated) with low B12 and/or folate; elevated MMA/homocysteine supportive; evaluate for pernicious anemia and malabsorption; neurologic exam

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; detected via CBC and symptom evaluation
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High when treated; neurologic recovery depends on early therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC widely available; MMA testing limited; B12/folate availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: B12 replacement (IM/PO depending on cause) and folate when indicated; treat underlying malabsorption
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed diagnosis of neurologic symptoms and limited confirmatory testing for pernicious anemia in some settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost effective treatment and broad CBC availability
